purpose: The master reference document for the India Trade & FDI/PLI analysis: every finding, figure, grade, and correction from the full research program in one place, with sources. This document is the source of truth from which the FDI pitch deck, dashboards, and bulletins can be updated -- when a number changes here, the derived outputs follow.
method: Compiled from the program's published bulletins, which rest on: direct extraction from DPIIT/RBI/ministry primary documents (annual reports, appendices, scheme portals); PIB Parliament-answer releases verified against release text with PRIDs; Parliamentary Standing Committee submissions (sansad.in); 20+ parallel research passes with adversarial verification; and independent cross-validation against Exim Bank of India and IBEF. Letter grades and traffic-light verdicts are stated judgments with criteria shown; every checkable claim carries its source.
executive_summary: India's FY2025-26 trade position: exports $441.7bn (+0.9% YoY), imports grew +7.4%, trade deficit $333.2bn -- widened 17.5% in a single year. Over 5 years imports grew 5.7x faster than exports. FDI equity inflow $58.85bn (+18% YoY); total FDI incl. reinvested earnings $88.3bn (Apr-Feb).

The PLI program's outcome numbers are large and compounding (Rs 2.16 lakh cr investment, Rs 20.41 lakh cr production, Rs 8.3 lakh cr exports, 14.39 lakh jobs as of 31 Dec 2025) -- but incentive DISBURSAL stands at Rs 28,748cr, roughly 15% of the outlay, and is concentrated in two sectors (Electronics and Pharma).

The export half of import substitution is demonstrably working in scheme-covered chapters (electronics exports +324% over 5yrs to $54bn; smartphones India's #1 export item in CY2025); the import half is not yet visible at chapter level (electronics imports also doubled to $104.9bn -- the input side of the export boom).

Sector verdicts (re-run on FY2025-26 FDI): 1 green (Pharma), 6 amber, 3 red. Both former greens fell -- Electronics FDI -43.5% and Medical Devices -40.4% against a national cycle up 18% -- while Pharma doubled (+114%) after Rs 5,433cr of disbursement, materially weakening the COVID-reshoring counter-explanation.

India's official investment-tracking data has documented, admitted defects: state-wise FDI is attributed by registered office (proven via Jio Platforms/Gujarat), the monthly IEM statistics series died with the NSWS migration, MoSPI's mega-project portal covers no private manufacturing, and no PLI ministry publishes beneficiary-level production status. AHIDF proves the capability exists (a nightly-refreshed, funnel-complete public dashboard) -- so the opacity is a choice.

Six ranked action items to further reduce the trade deficit: launch the chemicals/plastics PLI ($36.4bn uncovered imports), extend PLI Textiles to cotton apparel, deepen ECMS component localization, fix ACC Battery execution (zero disbursed), re-verify the Electronics/Medical-Devices FDI reversals next quarter, and mandate AHIDF-style funnels for every scheme.

headline_numbers.source: MOCI via Exim Bank May-2026 tracker; matches repo TRADESTAT tables

headline_numbers.change: more than double the next-largest, unmoved by 6 years of PLI

part_1_trade_position_fy2025_26.honest_reading: The export half of the import-substitution thesis is showing real progress in scheme-covered chapters -- the only declining export chapters are the cotton-apparel ones PLI structurally misses. The import half is not yet visible at chapter level: HS85 imports also doubled (components feeding the assembly boom), and total imports grew 5.7x faster than exports over five years. The trade-deficit lever is two-sided: deepen component localization where export momentum exists, and open schemes where the fastest-growing import bills have none.

part_2_sector_verdicts.criteria: Same criteria across both runs: did real DPIIT/RBI-tracked FDI equity inflow rise after the scheme's launch, distinguishable from the national cycle and from named confounders? Traffic lights: green = real, sustained, attributable rise; amber = mixed/confounded/too early; red = no lever or declining with no scheme.

verdicts_table.note: FDI nearly halved in FY26 while national FDI grew 18%; landed capacity real (Micron, CG Semi, Kaynes producing) but increasingly domestic-funded; ISM fund utilization was 23% (FY24) and 9% (FY25) per MeitY's Parliament submissions.
The 'cleanest positive result' reversed -- the concentration risk the original verdict flagged (a few MNC deals) materialized. 19 greenfield plants commissioned; production story ahead of FDI story.
Doubled to the series' highest level, landing AFTER Rs 5,433cr disbursement and investment at 237% of commitment -- the COVID-reshoring counter-explanation is now materially weaker.
Strong rebound but the FY21-22 lesson (one PE deal, +327%, fully reverted) demands deal tracing; ACC execution still 1 of 40 GWh commissioned, zero disbursed.
First meaningful post-launch rise, consistent with full subscription only landing Jan 2026 -- one year of evidence.
Still below pre-launch baseline; POSCO-JSW's $7.73bn moved Apr 2026 and lands in FY27 data -- next year is the real test. Capacity utilization dipped to 76%.
Proxy too coarse for YoY reads. C295 line operating; GE-HAL F414 terms finalized Apr 2026, signature pending. No production-incentive scheme; imports +80.9% (5yr).
Imports +86% to $14.2bn, no scheme. Chemicals PLI under formulation + Rs 600cr Chemical Parks budgeted FY26-27 -- a signal, not capacity.
Imports +45.8% to $22.2bn -- the largest no-scheme import bill. RRPCL (belongs to MoPNG, not DCPC) still short of financial close.

Cotton apparel exports still declining and still structurally outside PLI Textiles; the Oct 2025 amendment broadened HSN codes entirely within MMF scope. Ministry's own National Fibre Mission note admits the scheme 'focuses on downstream MMF fabrics and apparel' with fibre schemes operating 'in silos with limited convergence.'

part_2_sector_verdicts.control_sectors_note: Original-run control group (no-PLI sectors, FY19-FY25): Chemicals -46.5%, Rubber Goods (plastics proxy) -58.8%, Misc Mechanical (aircraft proxy) -5.5% -- against the six PLI sectors' +30.7% average, but that gap was carried by 2-3 sectors, not all six.

part_3_pli_program_assessment.outlay_inconsistency: PIB states the total program outlay as Rs 1.97 lakh cr (Year-End Review) and Rs 1.91 lakh cr (PRID 2230621) in different releases -- flagged, not reconciled.

scheme_report_card.disbursed: Rs 5,433cr (36% of outlay, Oct 2025, ministry AR)
scheme_report_card.evidence: Combined pharma-PLI sales Rs 3.19 lakh cr incl. Rs 2.04 lakh cr exports; 191 KSMs/DIs/APIs first-time produced in India; sales target already exceeded (PRID 2204595).

Production Rs 11.02 lakh cr (136% of target), exports Rs 6.21 lakh cr (127%); smartphones India's #1 export item CY2025 (Rs 2,62,452cr, PRID 2247771). Docked: only 2 of 5 domestic-category mobile applicants performing; jobs 92% of target. LSEM tenure closed 31 Mar 2026.

scheme_report_card.disbursed: ~Rs 54.81cr (0.8%, Dec 2025, secondary-sourced; FY25 flow Rs 22cr) -- grade revised A- -> B
scheme_report_card.evidence: Penicillin-G made in India again after ~30 years (Kakinada, Rs 1,910cr, Rs 2,700cr/yr import substitution) + Clavulanic Acid (Nalagarh); imports avoided Rs 3,591cr; 27 plants commissioned across 15+ named companies; capacity live for 26 of 33 products (~55,100 MT/yr).

scheme_report_card.disbursed: Rs 157.15cr to 7 applicants (4.6%, Dec 2025, LS reply PRID 2239576) -- grade revised B+ -> B
scheme_report_card.evidence: 19 greenfield projects commissioned; 44 products incl. linear accelerators, MRI, CT made in India for the first time; cumulative sales Rs 12,344cr incl. Rs 5,869cr exports (Sept 2025).

scheme_report_card.disbursed: ~Rs 1,175cr budget actuals through FY25 (~9.6%); FY26 RE Rs 1,944cr; Rs 331.86cr to 18 cos per LS Dec 2024
scheme_report_card.evidence: Sales >6x FY20; exports Rs 21,033cr; BSNL indigenous 4G stack deployed (PRID 2230621).
One of the few schemes paying at scale -- yet PIB names not a single beneficiary; the Britannia/ITC/Nestle names exist only on MoFPI's own PDF.

Component manufacturing 'has commenced' for compressors, motors, copper tubes, LED drivers (PRID 2230621); 30+ investors named. One applicant withdrew (Round 3); Round-3's 84 provisional quietly became 80 final with no explanation; no per-company production status public.

scheme_report_card.disbursed: ZERO BY DESIGN (payable 1yr post-commissioning; ~30 GW modules + 10.5 GW cells commissioned; LS reply Mar 2026) -- grade revised C+ -> B-
scheme_report_card.evidence: ~38,500 direct jobs by Jun 2025; awardees named (Reliance, First Solar, Waaree, Tata Power Solar...). Commitment data rich, execution/payout thin.
Sector turnover up 7x. A tiny scheme that broadly did its job.

Incremental sales Rs 32,879cr against a Rs 2,31,500cr projection; FY26-to-Dec sales Rs 13,126cr by 72 companies. 332nd Standing Committee: 'serious concern over the massive disconnect between the approved subsidy path and the actual meagre allocations and utilisations.'

PIB discloses 14 of 58 Round-1 projects WITHDREW -- attrition absent from steel.gov.in. 9,000 direct jobs real; capacity utilization industry-wide dipped to 76%; coking coal import dependence ~57 MMT/yr.

scheme_report_card.disbursed: Rs 54.5cr (0.5%, Aug 2025, PRID 2152545 -- to exactly 2 unnamed companies)
scheme_report_card.evidence: Only 26 of 74 R1-2 applicants had commenced production; zero beneficiary names in any PIB release; cotton apparel remains structurally excluded.

Ola's 1 GWh (of 40) in 'pilot production' since Mar 2024, 'working toward stabilizing operations for full-scale commercial production' per PIB. Hyundai Global Motors' original 20 GWh exited without 'withdrew' appearing in any government document; the freed capacity was re-tendered (10 GWh to Reliance R2, 10 GWh earmarked GSSS).

findings: Implementation exceeded same-year intent for the first time in 2025 (109.9%) -- reading as the 2021-22 intent surge (Rs 7.7 lakh cr proposed in 2021) maturing into implementation. Same-year flow ratio, not cohort-matched -- Part-B lags Part-A by months to decades.
Part-A filing counts fell steeply (1,267 -> 986 -> 702), consistent with the Apr 2025 eligibility-threshold raise (Rs 50cr -> Rs 125cr plant & machinery): fewer, larger IEMs.

The monthly IEM Statistics Report series is verifiably dead post-NSWS migration ('No Data Available' in DPIIT's own publications search); the annual Appendix series survives. NSWS itself publishes zero statistics. No investment-size band exists anywhere in the data, so a >Rs 150cr filter is impossible.
Revision warning: 2024's Part-B grew Rs ~2 lakh cr between the AR 2024-25 snapshot (to Nov) and AR 2025-26's full-year column -- partial-year IEM figures are heavily provisional.

Source: DPIIT Annual Report 2025-26, Appendices III-VI, Grand Total rows extracted from the PDF directly; a previously-generated derived workbook verified accurate on all 24 numbers with one footnote corrected (both parts run 'Up to Dec 2025').

fy2024_25_flow_split_rs_cr.source: Government data via PTI, 13 Jul 2025 -- the only per-scheme flow split found

india_two_tier_cost_of_capital.note: After 125bp of cuts through 2025; held 3 straight meetings into mid-2026 (61st MPC, 5 Jun 2026)

india_two_tier_cost_of_capital.tier: AAA corporate bonds 3-5yr (Tata Sons / Reliance tier)
india_two_tier_cost_of_capital.note: The cheapest domestic tier; AA- (Adani) pays 8.6-8.9%
Repo + spread; now 67.6% of the banking system's floating-rate book

financing_pattern_findings: Every target FDI-source country's policy rate sits below India's 5.25% repo -- from 4.25 points cheaper (Japan) to 1.6 (Saudi). Against India's ordinary-borrower tier the gap is 6-8.5 points; against the AAA tier it narrows to 1.5-3 for the US/UAE/Israel/Saudi.

The domestic-vs-FDI split follows TECHNOLOGY OWNERSHIP: where an Indian conglomerate has scale and doesn't need foreign IP (steel, chip packaging, battery cells), domestic AAA-tier self-funding is economically rational (Assam/Tata Electronics, Odisha/Tata Steel-Jindal, Gujarat gigafactories, Karnataka/JSW-Ola). Where the enabling technology is foreign-owned (compressors, appliance R&D, medical imaging, auto platforms), FDI is closer to a requirement (Andhra/Daikin-LG, Maharashtra's foreign OEM belt, Tamil Nadu/Samsung).

ICRA cross-check of the risk-absorption story is inconsistent: strongest for Defence (DPSU-vs-private financing asymmetry), moderate for Chemicals (diversification framing), a structural fact for White Goods (80% of RAC capacity in top-7 OEMs), weak for Pharma/Auto, EXPLICITLY ABSENT for Steel -- and an earlier automated pass's fabricated ICRA steel claim was caught against the source PDF and retracted in the published bulletin.

Exim Bank CEAT tracker (May-26 YoY): bank credit to Electronics +23.9%, Vehicles +26.4%, Pharma +22.6%, Iron & Steel +22.6% -- independent corroboration that domestic EBLR-priced credit is funding PLI-sector capacity.

entries.detail: Attributed by registered office, not factory location; DPIIT's own metadata says 'Documentation on methodology: Not applicable.' Proof: 9 of Gujarat's top-10 FY2020-21 deals were Jio Platforms (registered Ahmedabad), two years before the Semiconductor Policy. Assam registered $2.88M (FY25) then $2.37M (FY26) against a Rs 27,000cr Tata project -- domestic capital is structurally invisible to FDI data.

entries.verdict: Annual series alive, monthly dead, no size filter
entries.detail: See Part 3. The Rs 150cr threshold belongs to MoSPI's separate dataset; no shared key.

entries.verdict: Public infrastructure only -- zero coverage of the 10 priority sectors
entries.detail: 1,941 Central Sector projects, Rs 41.5 lakh cr revised cost, 15.64% implied cost overrun (computed -- MoSPI stopped publishing 'delayed' classifications after May 2025, a transparency regression). Steel category = SAIL upgrades only; 'Energy Storage' = pumped hydro. Useful only for enabling infrastructure around hubs (Dholera airport/transmission).

entries.verdict: The counter-example: transparency is a choice
entries.detail: Public no-login Power BI, refreshed nightly; state/district/bank-wise; full funnel incl. rejections (11,026 applications -> 1,592 eligible -> 910 sanctioned -> 357 disbursed, ~96% never reach disbursement). Ceiling: aggregates only, stops at 'loan disbursed' -- one gate short of 'project operational.' Also: an unresolved Rs 500cr outlay discrepancy between DAHD's page and the Cabinet announcement.

entries.verdict: Best current source for disbursal + company names -- with sector-specific blind spots
entries.detail: Parliament-answer releases carry the freshest scheme figures. But PIB names zero companies for Textiles/Steel/Food Processing, and inauguration releases sometimes name only sites, not companies.
Government institution, separate publishing chain; monthly trade/FDI and sector-credit trackers. Deficit figure cross-validated the repo's computation within 0.3%.

pharmaceuticals.gov.in is NXDOMAIN (now pharma-dept.gov.in); makeinindia.gov.in does not exist (makeinindia.com); the legacy DPIIT SIA-statistics route is a dead SPA shell; meity.gov.in and pib.gov.in 403 plain fetches (browser UA works).

part_7_action_items.action: Launch the chemicals/petrochemicals PLI covering Inorganic Chemicals (HS28) and Plastics (HS39)
part_7_action_items.justification: Combined $36.4bn FY26 imports growing 46-86% over 5yrs, zero coverage -- the two largest open gaps.

part_7_action_items.action: Extend PLI Textiles to cotton-based apparel (HS61/62)
part_7_action_items.justification: The only declining export chapters sit in the scheme's structural blind spot; Oct 2025 amendment broadened MMF codes only.

part_7_action_items.action: Deepen electronics COMPONENT localization through ECMS
part_7_action_items.justification: HS85 exports +324% but imports +101.5% to $104.9bn; ECMS (Rs 22,919cr, open) decides whether the electronics deficit narrows.

part_7_action_items.action: Fix ACC Battery PLI execution before adding new capacity schemes
part_7_action_items.justification: 1 of 40 GWh commissioned, zero disbursed, one awardee quietly gone; the Standing Committee's demanded beneficiary-wise review is the vehicle.

part_7_action_items.action: Re-verify the Electronics and Medical Devices FDI reversals next quarter
part_7_action_items.justification: Both former greens fell 40%+ against a rising cycle; one year warrants amber, two would be a trend.

part_7_action_items.action: Publish AHIDF-style application-to-disbursement funnels for every PLI scheme
part_7_action_items.justification: Capability proven inside government; scheme transparency exposes where approved capacity is stuck.

part_8_corrections_ledger: ~74% China API-dependency figure: not corroborated in DoP's own Annual Report; re-attribute or drop.
RRPCL belongs to MoPNG (IOCL/BPCL/HPCL JV), not DCPC; still no financial close.
GE-HAL F414: 'terms finalized Apr 2026, signature pending' -- not 'not signed', not 'signed'.
Electronics disbursal Rs 15,554cr (~Mar 2026) supersedes Rs 6,834cr (Aug 2024); Auto Rs 2,377.56cr supersedes Rs 1,350.83cr.
The Rs 28,748cr cross-scheme disbursal figure comes from Parliament-answer PRID 2230621, not the DPIIT Year-End Review.
Specialty Steel: 14 of 58 Round-1 projects withdrew (PIB-disclosed, absent from steel.gov.in).
IEM 2025 footnote: both Part A and Part B run 'Up to Dec 2025' (an earlier workbook said Part B to Nov).
Prior sector verdicts were set on FY2024-25 data; the FY2025-26 re-run moved three (documented, original kept as dated baseline).
An automated pass fabricated an ICRA steel risk-differentiation claim; caught against the source PDF and retracted in the published bulletin.
PIB outlay inconsistency (Rs 1.97 vs 1.91 lakh cr) and DAHD/Cabinet AHIDF outlay discrepancy (Rs 29,110 vs 29,610cr): flagged, unreconciled.

Report-card grades revised on Mar-2026 Parliament answers: Bulk Drugs A-->B, Medical Devices B+->B, Solar C+->B- (zero by design, 30 GW commissioned); Telecom payout evidence attached (~Rs 1,175cr through FY25).

part_9_infrastructure_and_maintenance.published_outputs: 13 bulletins (charts/ + data/), the 22-slide FDI pitch deck (PPTX/PDF), a 1080x1080 social post, tracked quarterly PDF snapshots (reports/published/), and this master report.
tools: scripts/report_synthesizer.py -- schema-agnostic JSON -> PDF/DOCX/PPTX (pixel-faithful PDF via headless Chrome when paired with chart HTML); --manifest batch mode over data/repo_manifest_2026-07-19.json.

templates/ -- slide deck, dashboard, report card, and document templates, each with pre-built outputs; build_deck.sh packages the headless-Chrome -> PNG-per-slide -> python-pptx -> LibreOffice pipeline with a --dashboard mode.

.github/workflows/quarterly-dashboard-refresh.yml -- quarterly PDF snapshot + auto-opened re-verification issue from .github/QUARTERLY_CHECKLIST.md (now covering 9 ministries, PIB aggregates, Exim trackers, IEM, MoSPI, and rate benchmarks).
next_refresh_priorities: Re-verify Electronics and Medical Devices FDI against a second year of data.

Update disbursal grades against new Parliament-answer PRIDs; fill the four disbursal gaps (Bulk Drugs, Medical Devices, Telecom, Solar PV) via Lok Sabha/Rajya Sabha answers and ministry annual reports -- research pass in flight as of this document.
Re-extract sector/state splits from the next quarterly FDI factsheet; check the updated FDI metadata document (27 Feb 2026) against the 'methodology: Not applicable' version.
Watch: chemicals PLI Cabinet approval, PLI Textiles cotton broadening, ACC GSSS re-tender, POSCO-JSW cash landing in FY27 FDI data, Semicon 2.0 approvals.

All-India 2021-2025: Rs 19,51,828cr across 4,859 implemented IEMs; top 3 states = 63.3%.
